name: Stuffed Cabbage with Rice & Pine Nuts Avgolem
recipeInstructions:
1. Plunge cabbage heads into boiling salted water and cook for about 8 minutes. Drain thoroughly and set aside.
2. Prepare the filling: In a heavy skillet, heat clarified butter. Add chopped onions and cook until soft and transparent.
3. Add water and bring to a boil. Then add rice and stir. Lower heat and simmer gently until rice absorbs liquid (approximately 15 minutes).
4. Remove from heat and add raisins or currants, pine nuts, parsley, dill, salt, and pepper. Cool mixture.
5. Separate two eggs; mix egg whites into the filling. Reserve yolks for avgolemono sauce.
6. Stuff cabbage leaves with one heaping tablespoon of filling, roll tightly, and place seam side down in a casserole dish.
7. Dot rolls with butter, add water to cover, then cover cabbage rolls with an inverted plate and return to casserole.
8. Simmer for approximately 1 hour, then transfer to a warm serving dish and keep warm.
9. Strain remaining liquid for avgolemono sauce. Beat remaining eggs and yolks for 2 minutes.
10. Gradually add lemon juice, continuing to beat. Add cooking liquid by droplets, beating steadily until all is incorporated.
11. Cook over hot water, not boiling, stirring constantly until sauce thickens enough to coat a spoon. Pour over cabbage rolls and serve hot.
